# codex-acp v0.27.1

`v0.27.1` is the publishable Linux-only follow-up to the `v0.27.0` compatibility
candidate. The `v0.27.0` tag did not produce a GitHub Release because its
unsupported Windows matrix job failed inside the pinned upstream
`codex-utils-pty` dependency; no `v0.27.0` release assets were published.

## Compatibility release highlights

- Updated the coordinated Codex dependency set to the official
  `openai/codex` `rust-v0.144.6` baseline.
- Kept reasoning effort forward-compatible with `max`, `ultra`, and future
  non-empty values.
- Added actionable, read-only startup diagnostics with adapter, backend,
  schema, executable, and config-path identity.
- Removed adapter-side full-buffer writeback after Codex edits, leaving editor
  reconciliation to Zed's native file watcher.
- Tightened missing-session recovery, app-server request rejection, model
  discovery, selector configuration, and account-limit status summaries.
- Prevented background transport draining from consuming notifications owned by
  an active turn.
- Preserved the real error from a discovered-path resume retry instead of
  silently treating authentication, configuration, or permission failures as a
  missing session.
- Reduced the `Status` selector to working account-limit variants and the
  compaction action; full session, MCP, skills, and plugins details remain in
  `/status`.

## Release platform policy

- Official prebuilt releases currently target Linux x86_64 GNU only.
- The public job and artifact names use `Linux x86_64 GNU` and
  `codex-acp-linux-x86_64-gnu`. Cargo still uses its canonical internal target
  name, `x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`.
- macOS and Windows binary releases are deferred until the project has real
  maintainers and runtime coverage for those platforms.
- Users who need macOS or Windows may attempt to build from source and maintain
  any required platform-specific fixes themselves; those builds are not part
  of this release's support contract.
- Push/PR CI now performs formatting, lint, release-target tests, and an
  installer smoke test without uploading a second release-like artifact.

## Install

Download the release installer and pin this version:

```bash
curl -fsSLO https://github.com/Qweasd123tg/zed-codex-ACP-CAS/releases/latest/download/install.sh
bash install.sh --download 0.27.1
```

The installer verifies the matching checksum, smoke-tests the binary before
activation, installs it atomically to `~/.local/bin/codex-acp`, writes
`codex-acp.build-info.txt`, and creates the adapter config home when needed.

After upgrading, restart the external-agent process or the Zed session. If
Zed's GUI environment cannot resolve the backend `codex` executable from
`PATH`, set `CODEX_ACP_CODEX_BIN` to its absolute path in the custom agent
environment.

## Verification

The release candidate is validated with:

```text
cargo fmt --all -- --check
cargo test
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
cargo test --release --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
cargo build --release --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
git diff --check
bash script/build_local_release.sh
./install.sh
./install.sh --from-binary target/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/release/codex-acp --config-home /tmp/codex-acp-release-config /tmp/codex-acp-release codex-acp
```

The GitHub release publishes `codex-acp-linux-x86_64-gnu` with its matching
`.sha256`, plus `install.sh` and `install.sh.sha256`.
